requiredDocuments: Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN) or Social Security Number
prerequisites: Must be registered with the Alabama Secretary of State if operating as an LLC, corporation, or partnership
Must have a valid physical address in Alabama
Must comply with local zoning requirements
licenses.processingTimeline: Same-day to 5 business days for online applications; up to 10 business days for paper applications
applicationProcess: Determine the correct license category for your business type at the Alabama Department of Revenue website
Register your business entity with the Alabama Secretary of State if required
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS at irs.gov
Visit your county probate office or apply online at MyAlabamaTaxes to complete the application
Pay the applicable license fee based on your business type and gross receipts
Display your license at your principal place of business once received
licenses.notes: Alabama requires a business privilege license from each county in which the business operates. Some business types require additional state-level licenses.

requiredDocuments: Business name registration or DBA filing (if applicable)
Federal EIN or Social Security Number
Proof of liability insurance (recommended, may be required by clients)
prerequisites: Register business name with Alabama Secretary of State or county probate office if using a trade name
Obtain general liability insurance (strongly recommended; minimum $1 million per occurrence)
If employing workers, register for Alabama withholding tax
applicationProcess: Choose and register your business structure (sole proprietor, LLC, etc.) with the Alabama Secretary of State
File a DBA ('doing business as') with your county probate office if operating under a trade name
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS
Purchase general liability insurance for your cleaning business
Apply for an Alabama Business Privilege License at your county probate office or online
If hiring employees, register for Alabama unemployment insurance with the Alabama Department of Labor
Renew your license annually before October 31
licenses.notes: Alabama does not require a separate state-issued cleaning license; the county business privilege license covers cleaning services. Some cities may require additional local business licenses.

licenses.licenseName: Alabama Food Service Establishment Permit (Mobile Unit)
licenses.issuingAgency: Alabama Department of Public Health – Environmental Health
licenses.applicationFee: $200 – $400 (state health permit); plus county business privilege license $15–$150; plus local permits vary
requiredDocuments: Commissary agreement letter (agreement with a licensed commercial kitchen for food prep and cleaning)
Menu of all food items to be sold
prerequisites: Must have a commissary agreement with a licensed commercial kitchen
Vehicle must pass a health inspection
At least one food handler must hold a valid food manager certification
Must comply with local zoning and vending location ordinances
licenses.processingTimeline: 2 – 4 weeks after submitting all documents and passing inspection
applicationProcess: Register your business entity with the Alabama Secretary of State
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS
Secure a commissary agreement with a licensed commercial kitchen in Alabama
Ensure your food truck vehicle/trailer meets all equipment requirements (three-compartment sink, hand-washing sink, adequate refrigeration)
Submit the Food Service Establishment application to your county Environmental Health office along with all required documents
Schedule and pass a health inspection of your food truck
Obtain a county Business Privilege License from your county probate office
Check with each city or municipality where you plan to operate for any additional local vending permits
Display all permits visibly inside your food truck during operation
licenses.notes: Food trucks operating in multiple counties must comply with the health regulations of each county. Some cities such as Birmingham and Huntsville have additional mobile food vendor permit requirements and designated vending zones.
